en.extract:
The silver-backed butcherbird (Cracticus argenteus) is a small, shrike-like bird. It is almost identical to the grey butcherbird (C. torquatus) of which it considered by some authorities to be a subspecies, C. torquatus argenteus.
The species was first described by ornithologist John Gould in 1836 as Cracticus argenteus.


Description
The silver-backed butcherbird looks similar to the Grey butcherbird, except its back is lighter and silvery.
This species is snappy and fearless, diving at humans and dogs near the nest. It may attack other birds like the spangled drongo or common koel.


Diet
It is a part-time predator but insects are also taken. It feeds on small lizards such as skinks and mice along with small birds that it snares and plucks out of the sky.


References

The '''silver-backed butcherbird''' ('''''Cracticus argenteus''''') is a small, [[shrike]]-like bird. It is almost identical to the [[grey butcherbird]] (''C. torquatus'') of which it considered by some authorities to be a subspecies, ''C. torquatus argenteus''.<ref>{{avibase|6BFC126268C28F71|Cracticus argenteus}}</ref>

The species was first described by ornithologist [[John Gould]] in 1836 as ''Cracticus argenteus''.<ref>{{cite journal|last=Gould|first=John|author-link=John Gould|title=Characters of new species of Birds from New South Wales|journal=Proceedings of the Zoological Society of London|year=1836|page=126|url=https://archive.org/stream/proceedingsofgen36zool#page/126/mode/2up/search/Cracticus|access-date=14 July 2010}}</ref>

== Description ==
The silver-backed butcherbird looks similar to the Grey butcherbird, except its back is lighter and silvery.<ref name=morcombe>{{cite book|title=Field guide to Australian birds|first=Michael|last=Morcombe|publisher=Steve Parish Publishing|year=2004}}</ref>
This species is snappy and fearless, diving at humans and dogs near the nest. It may attack other birds like the [[spangled drongo]] or [[common koel]].

== Diet ==
It is a part-time [[predation|predator]] but insects are also taken. It feeds on small lizards such as [[skink]]s and mice along with small birds that it snares and plucks out of the sky.
